specificity.note: Names the statute (EnEfG §11, PUE ≤1.2, mid-2026 commencement), quantified frictions (24–36-month Frankfurt grid waits, 6.3% vacancy, 83% pre-let), and the exact ledger nodes with values (geo.DE 0.50, obtainableFactor 0.03) plus dated perturbations. Actors (Vantage, NTT GDC, TenneT/Amprion) and instruments are all identified.

nonObviousness.note: The core inversion — that record-low vacancy and 83% pre-let mean NO replacement-demand slack to offset new-build slips, per whyMissable — is a genuine reframe of data the founder would file as upside, and it names new reasons (EnEfG rework, grid queues) rather than a band-edge claim. Not a 4 because German grid constraints and EnEfG are well-publicized headwinds a diligent founder tracking Frankfurt would plausibly have flagged.

mechanismDepth.note: Three-step chain (mandates+queues → fit-out/energization slip → smaller/slower Year-1 orderable slice) with refs at each hop and the key insight that PDU revenue recognizes at fit-out, not announcement. The final hop — design rework translating to 12-month PDU order slippage — remains inference, as the rationale itself concedes ('an inference rather than directly observed slippage').

evidenceQuality.note: The key premises are multiply corroborated (statutory §11 text attached, neospaces + igorslab/Mainova on grid waits, JLL on vacancy/pre-let), and the risk honestly logs the contradicting anwalt.de source on the pending novelle and bounds its likelihood revision accordingly. The novelle could postpone the load-bearing regulatory trigger, which keeps this below a 4.

decisionRelevance.note: Mitigations directly change next-two-quarter sequencing and spend: reweight Year-1 pipeline to NL/PL, build a retrofit offer insensitive to energization slips, and a dated order-timing survey with voiNodeId geo.DE. Indicators (≥2 flagship 6+ month slips; BfEE/BAFA waiver practice) are concrete and monitorable.
